meta.title: Identity UEBA — MITRE ATT&CK Full Mapping

anomaly_mappings.description: Same user authenticates from two cities too far apart for the time elapsed
anomaly_mappings.notes: Speed threshold typically ~900 km/h. Sub-technique depends on account type: T1078.002 (Domain Accounts) for AD, T1078.004 (Cloud Accounts) for SaaS.

anomaly_mappings.description: User logs in from a country they have never visited before
anomaly_mappings.notes: High signal if combined with odd hour. Requires user geo-history baseline.

anomaly_mappings.description: User logs in from an unfamiliar city within an otherwise known country
anomaly_mappings.notes: Lower severity than new country. Could be legitimate travel.

anomaly_mappings.description: Login from new country during user's typical sleeping hours
anomaly_mappings.notes: Strong signal — stolen creds resold internationally. Combine geography + temporal context.

anomaly_mappings.description: User moves between countries at unusual speed (not quite impossible, but suspicious)
anomaly_mappings.notes: e.g., 3 countries in 6 hours. Below impossible travel threshold but still anomalous.

anomaly_mappings.description: Login from a sanctioned or high-risk country (e.g., Russia, North Korea, Iran)
anomaly_mappings.notes: Requires geo-threat intelligence feed. Escalate immediately if paired with privileged account.

anomaly_mappings.description: Authentication from a known Tor exit node
anomaly_mappings.notes: Anonymization flag. Compare against user's VPN usage baseline.

anomaly_mappings.description: Login from a cloud or datacenter IP range not associated with the user's org
anomaly_mappings.notes: Common for attackers using VPS proxies. Cross-reference with known org IP ranges.

anomaly_mappings.description: GPS coordinates inconsistent with claimed city (spoofed location)
anomaly_mappings.notes: Requires device GPS data. May indicate location spoofing via emulator or VPN.

anomaly_mappings.description: Rapid cycling through IPs/ports — scanning or proxy rotation
anomaly_mappings.notes: May indicate C2 infrastructure or credential stuffing infrastructure.

anomaly_mappings.description: Login during unusual hours for the user (single event)
anomaly_mappings.notes: Check user's local timezone. Single event is low severity; sustained is higher.

anomaly_mappings.description: Repeated off-hours access over multiple days
anomaly_mappings.notes: Insider threat pattern. May indicate account sharing or compromised credentials.

anomaly_mappings.description: First-time login on a weekend or public holiday
anomaly_mappings.notes: Escalates when paired with data access. Requires calendar data.

anomaly_mappings.description: User logged in from a country on a date when the user's home country celebrates a holiday
anomaly_mappings.notes: User 'should' be at home. Strong social signal.

anomaly_mappings.description: User logging in much more frequently than their historical baseline
anomaly_mappings.notes: Automation or scripted access. Compare against rolling window baseline.

anomaly_mappings.description: No further activity from the user after a suspicious event
anomaly_mappings.notes: Could indicate session hijack then exit. Post-hoc detection.

anomaly_mappings.description: User's event timestamps deviate from their typical hourly pattern
anomaly_mappings.notes: Suggests account shared across timezones. Gradual drift over weeks.

anomaly_mappings.description: Multiple rapid failed logins targeting one account
anomaly_mappings.notes: Lockout threshold may be hit. Classic brute force.

anomaly_mappings.description: Single password tried across many accounts
anomaly_mappings.notes: Common AD attack — avoids lockout. Look for same password hash across accounts.

anomaly_mappings.description: Known breached credentials used at scale
anomaly_mappings.notes: Often from dark web credential dumps. Cross-reference with HaveIBeenPwned.

anomaly_mappings.description: Sudden increase in failed-to-successful login ratio
anomaly_mappings.notes: Early indicator. Monitor ratio against rolling baseline.

anomaly_mappings.description: User denies multiple MFA push requests
anomaly_mappings.notes: MFA fatigue attack. Followed by eventual approval = critical.

anomaly_mappings.description: Successful login immediately following a chain of failures
anomaly_mappings.notes: Indicates attacker finally guessed correctly. Investigate subsequent activity.

anomaly_mappings.description: User switches to backup authentication after primary fails
anomaly_mappings.notes: Could indicate credential theft. Legitimate users rarely switch methods.

anomaly_mappings.description: Password reset then immediate login from new device/location
anomaly_mappings.notes: Attacker uses self-service reset. Compare reset requestor IP with login IP.

anomaly_mappings.description: First-seen device authenticating
anomaly_mappings.notes: Low signal alone, high with other factors. Requires device registry.

anomaly_mappings.description: Unfamiliar user-agent string
anomaly_mappings.notes: e.g., Chrome to Tor Browser. Monitor user-agent changes.

anomaly_mappings.description: First-seen device connecting through a VPN
anomaly_mappings.notes: Strong stolen-credential signal. New device + anonymization = suspicious.

anomaly_mappings.description: Device fingerprint mismatch (claimed vs actual OS, screen, fonts)
anomaly_mappings.notes: Anti-fingerprinting evasion. Compare passive vs active fingerprint.

anomaly_mappings.description: Login from Android emulator, iOS simulator, or VM
anomaly_mappings.notes: Common in bot/fraud networks. Check for emulator artifacts.

anomaly_mappings.description: Device security policy violation
anomaly_mappings.notes: Policy enforcement. Root detection / SafetyNet / jailbreak check.

anomaly_mappings.description: User accessing via private/incognito browsing
anomaly_mappings.notes: Low severity; mainly log. No direct MITRE mapping.

anomaly_mappings.description: Session cookie used from a different device/IP
anomaly_mappings.notes: Token theft. Compare cookie issuance IP vs usage IP.

anomaly_mappings.description: Login from a device running unpatched/unusual OS
anomaly_mappings.notes: Policy violation, lateral movement vector. OS version check.

anomaly_mappings.description: Client certificate expired, revoked, or mismatched
anomaly_mappings.notes: Certificate theft. Check revocation status.

anomaly_mappings.description: Large data volume to a previously unseen IP
anomaly_mappings.notes: C2 or staged exfil. Size + new IP = strong signal.

anomaly_mappings.description: Large data transfer to a new country
anomaly_mappings.notes: Geopolitical risk. Cross-reference destination country risk score.

anomaly_mappings.description: Large download during non-business hours
anomaly_mappings.notes: Insider threat. Compare volume against user baseline.

anomaly_mappings.description: Sudden increase in data transfer vs user baseline
anomaly_mappings.notes: Statistical anomaly. Z-score or MAD-based threshold.

anomaly_mappings.description: Transfer to personal cloud storage (Google Drive, Dropbox, etc.)
anomaly_mappings.notes: Insider exfiltration vector. Check if destination domain matches known personal cloud.

anomaly_mappings.description: Sending data to external email addresses
anomaly_mappings.suspected_technique: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration Over Email
anomaly_mappings.notes: Corporate email to personal email. Attachment size anomaly.

anomaly_mappings.description: User creating archive files and uploading in unusual volumes
anomaly_mappings.notes: Data staging before exfil. Look for zip/rar/7z creation by non-admin users.

anomaly_mappings.description: Unusual increase in print jobs before resignation
anomaly_mappings.notes: Insider threat — documents leaving company physically. Integrate with HR data.

anomaly_mappings.description: Rapid file copy to removable media
anomaly_mappings.notes: Policy violation. USB device history and file operation rate.

anomaly_mappings.description: Account suddenly granted admin/privileged access
anomaly_mappings.notes: Privilege escalation. Monitor for unexpected group membership changes.

anomaly_mappings.description: User added/removed from security groups
anomaly_mappings.notes: Lateral movement prep. Compare with manager approval.

anomaly_mappings.description: Creation of a new privileged account
anomaly_mappings.notes: Persistence. New accounts with admin privileges outside normal HR process.

anomaly_mappings.description: New service principal or app registration
anomaly_mappings.notes: Backdoor/OAuth abuse. Monitor Entra ID / Azure AD app registrations.

anomaly_mappings.description: User assumes an IAM role they have never used before
anomaly_mappings.notes: Cloud-specific (AWS STS). Role assumption history baseline.

anomaly_mappings.description: Unusual API key or access key generation
anomaly_mappings.notes: Persistence/automation. Key creation outside normal DevOps workflow.

anomaly_mappings.description: Account inactive >90 days suddenly active
anomaly_mappings.notes: Stale account compromise. Combine with off-hours or new geo for escalation.

anomaly_mappings.description: Regular user unexpectedly able to perform admin actions
anomaly_mappings.notes: Cloud misconfiguration abuse. Compare effective permissions vs assigned.

anomaly_mappings.description: User accessing resources in another tenant
anomaly_mappings.notes: Multi-tenant attack. B2B collaboration abuse.

anomaly_mappings.description: Same account active in multiple distinct locations simultaneously
anomaly_mappings.notes: Session hijack. Geo-distance between concurrent sessions = 0 time diff.

anomaly_mappings.description: Same session/JWT used from different IPs
anomaly_mappings.notes: Token theft. Compare jti (JWT ID) or session ID across events.

anomaly_mappings.description: Events from same session in geographically impossible sequence
anomaly_mappings.notes: Session doppelganger. Same session ID, different hemispheres in minutes.

anomaly_mappings.description: Session token living longer than typical TTL
anomaly_mappings.notes: Token misuse. Token refresh patterns vs expected expiry.

anomaly_mappings.description: Refresh token used from unexpected location
anomaly_mappings.notes: OAuth abuse. Refresh token issuance vs usage geo.

anomaly_mappings.description: User-agent shows browser but auth protocol is non-interactive (e.g., ROPC)
anomaly_mappings.notes: Automation misusing browser profile. Check OAuth grant type.

anomaly_mappings.description: User accessing an application they have never used before
anomaly_mappings.notes: Recon/lateral movement. Application access history baseline.

anomaly_mappings.description: User accessing unusual servers, shares, or databases
anomaly_mappings.notes: Data discovery. Unusual SMB/NFS/SQL connections.

anomaly_mappings.description: Unusually complex or voluminous database queries
anomaly_mappings.notes: Data scraping. Query complexity, row count, SELECT * without WHERE.

anomaly_mappings.description: Service account behaving unlike its historical baseline
anomaly_mappings.notes: Compromised service principal. API call type, rate, and target drift.

anomaly_mappings.description: User's browser/app version changing abruptly
anomaly_mappings.notes: Possible device change. No direct MITRE mapping.

anomaly_mappings.description: User's typical login time shifting by >2 hours over several days
anomaly_mappings.notes: Could indicate shared account. Gradual shift vs abrupt change.

anomaly_mappings.description: Navigation pattern differs from user's typical workflows
anomaly_mappings.notes: RPA/bot activity. Requires UI analytics integration.

anomaly_mappings.description: Keystroke dynamics or mouse movement anomaly
anomaly_mappings.notes: Account takeover. Continuous authentication signal.

anomaly_mappings.description: Unusual data collection activity before resignation
anomaly_mappings.notes: Insider threat indicator. Integrate with HR resignation date.

anomaly_mappings.description: Anomalous activity spike within N days of resignation
anomaly_mappings.notes: IP theft window. Compare activity against user's own baseline, not team.

anomaly_mappings.description: Access to data user has no business need for
anomaly_mappings.notes: GDPR/HIPAA compliance. Data classification labels + RBAC audit.

anomaly_mappings.description: Contractor/partner accessing internal systems unusually
anomaly_mappings.notes: Third-party risk. Compare contractor activity vs contract scope.

anomaly_mappings.description: User enumerating directory users, groups, or permissions
anomaly_mappings.notes: Lateral movement prep. LDAP queries, net group, Azure AD enumeration.

anomaly_mappings.description: User listing/downloading all files in a repository
anomaly_mappings.notes: Data staging. High API call rate to SharePoint/OneDrive/Docs.

anomaly_mappings.description: User fails MFA multiple times then succeeds
anomaly_mappings.notes: MFA bypass attempt. Monitor MFA denial chain.
